steps.title: Find the subject
steps.content: The subject is "Anthropologist Livia Barbosa" — a single person, third-person singular. The long phrase "of the Universidade Federal Fluminense in Brazil" is a prepositional modifier and does not affect the subject's number.

steps.title: Choose the verb that agrees
steps.content: Rule: a singular third-person subject takes a singular present-tense verb (adds -s). A. "study" — base/plural form. Fails: used with plural subjects ("they study"), not a singular noun like "Barbosa." B. "are studying" — plural present progressive ("are" signals plural). Fails: "Barbosa are studying" violates agreement. C. "studies" — singular present tense (-s ending). Matches the singular subject "Anthropologist Livia Barbosa." Correct. D. "have studied" — plural present perfect ("have" signals plural). Fails: "Barbosa have studied" violates agreement. That matches choice C — C is correct.
